data.title: Top court in Brazil rules that social media platforms will bear liability for users' posts
data.description:
Brazil’s supreme court has ruled that social media platforms can be held legally responsible for posts that promote hate speech, violence, or “anti-democratic acts,” and companies like Facebook (NASDAQ:META), TikTok (BDNCE), and X will have to remove such material even without a takedown order, according to a report by the Financial Times.

The move by the South American nation's top court on Thursday has paved the way for individuals to be able to sue digital platforms if illegal content that is brought to the companies’ attention is not deleted, the report said.

“We are concerned about the implications of the ruling on speech and the millions of businesses that rely on our apps for growth and job creation in Brazil,” Meta reportedly said. “[It] brings legal uncertainties and will have significant consequences for free expression, innovation, and digital economic development, significantly increasing the risk of doing business in Brazil.”

Under a previous rule, apps and sites were only deemed civilly liable for damages caused by third-party posts when they failed to comply with judicial orders to take down the offending content.

Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) reportedly said it had already expressed “concerns about changes that could impact freedom of expression and the digital economy,” adding that it was analyzing the decision.

data.title: Alibaba releases image generating AI model
data.description:
Alibaba Group (NYSE:BABA) has released an image generating artificial intelligence model, Qwen VLo, which will compete with other image generators such as OpenAI's 4o and Google's (GOOG)(GOOGL) Imagen 4.

The Hangzhou, China-based e-commerce giant also provides cloud services and continues to build on its AI offerings. It is currently working with Apple (AAPL) to bring Apple Intelligence to the mainland.

"This newly upgraded model not only 'understands' the world but also generates high-quality recreations based on that understanding, truly bridging the gap between perception and creation," said the Qwen team in a blog post. "Note that this is a preview version and you can access it through Qwen Chat. You can directly send a prompt like 'Generate a picture of a cute cat' to generate an image or upload an image of a cat and ask 'Add a cap on the cat's head' to modify an image."

The model is intended to have more precise content understanding and support for open-ended instruction-based editing. It can understand English and Chinese.

"Qwen VLo is capable of directly generating images and modifying them by replacing backgrounds, adding subjects, performing style transfers, and even executing extensive modifications based on open-ended instructions, as well as handling detection and segmentation tasks," the company said.

data.title: Apple EU-mandated App Store changes likely to have minimal financial impact: Evercore
data.description:
The changed that Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) made to its App Store policy to comply with orders from the European Union are likely to have a minimal financial impact, investment firm Evercore ISI said.

“EU countries account for [roughly] 8% of total App Store revenue, and we estimate they generated [roughly] $5.7B in total App Store sales in 2023,” Evercore analyst Amit Daryanani wrote in a note to clients. “Assuming a 20% take rate, this would imply Apple earned about $1.2B in revenue from the EU App Store. Even if we assume every developer chooses to side load and use third party payments and that $1.2B in revenue goes to $0, we still estimate that would only have a $0.06 impact to EPS (~1% impact). Assuming the fee goes down to 10%, this would imply an impact of ~$570M and [less than] 1% of EPS.”

Daryanani has an Outperform rating and $250 price target on Apple.

Apple announced changes on Thursday to its App Store for users in the EU to comply with the continent's Digital Markets Act and avoid escalating financial penalties. Developers with apps in the EU can promote offers for purchase of digital goods or services available at a destination of their choice.

The new rules give developers two payment tiers, including a basic one with a 5% fee and a second tier with increased functionality that has a 13% fee.

Apple also introduced a “Core Technology Commission,” a 5% fee charged on external transactions, which replaces the prior $0.50 fee per download.

data.title: EU and US expect to reach a tariff deal by July deadline - report
data.description:
The European Union and the U.S. are confident they'll reach some form of agreement before a U.S.-imposed July 9 deadline that will avert the 50% tariff on EU goods and the countermeasures the EU has threatened, according to a media report on Friday.

Behind closed doors, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen told EU leaders that she believed a deal could be reached by the deadline to head off an economically damaging escalation in trade tensions, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Von der Leyen said the Trump administration had presented a new proposal this week, changing the tone of the discussion among the leaders. Many were prepared to accept some degree of trade imbalance to stem an escalation, the report said.

On the U.S. side, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said in a Bloomberg Television interview that the EU had increased the pace of negotiations in recent weeks. "Europe has done an excellent job, they're working hard," he said. "I'm optimistic — I think we can get a deal now."

The intensifying negotiations have included in-depth talks on steel and aluminum, automobiles, pharmaceuticals, semiconductors, and civilian aircraft, Bloomberg said, regarding both tariff and non-tariff barriers. 

As recently as Thursday, von der Leyen told reporters that "all options remain on the table."

The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) edged up 0.1% in U.S. midday trading on Friday. The S&P 500 Index rose 0.6%. The Stoxx Europe 600 Index continuous contract rose 1.0% at 5:16 PM CEDT. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: USD and stocks are historically muted after a new Fed chair is nominated
data.description:
U.S. dollar (DXY) and S&P 500 (SP500) performance is usually muted or lower three months after a Federal Reserve chair nomination, according to data from Bank of America.

President Donald Trump is expected to nominate a new Fed chair as Jerome Powell’s term is set to end in May of next year, and during the three months after a Fed chair nomination the 2-year U.S. Treasury (US2Y) has been up an average of 65 basis points, and the 10-year yield (US10Y) has been up an average of 49 basis points, during the last seven nominations since 1970, with chairs Arthur Burns, William Miller, Paul Volcker, Alan Greenspan, Ben Bernanke, Janet Yellen, and Jerome Powell.

 

 

On the other hand, the U.S. dollar (DXY) has been muted or lower three months after the last seven nominations: -0.3% after Burns, 0.9% after Miller, -0.6% after Volcker, -4.4% after Greenspan, -5.7% after Bernanke, -0.5% after Yellen, and 0.7% after Powell.

Here are the S&P 500 (SP500) performances three months after each chair nomination:

  • Arthur Burns -14.3%
  • William Miller 9.1%
  • Paul Volcker 0.5%
  • Alan Greenspan -5.3%
  • Ben Bernanke -0.9%
  • Janet Yellen 3.6%
  • Jerome Powell 1.6%

A bubble risk is high for the second half of 2025 as the U.S. pivots from tariff policy risks to tax cuts and rate cuts “to incite U.S. dollar (DXY) devaluation” and a “U.S. stock (SP500), (DJI), (COMP:IND) bubble as cure to reduce U.S. debt burden,” said Michael Hartnett, chief investment strategist for Bank of America, in a Friday note.

He recommended being long U.S. growth stocks (SPYG), (VOOG), (IVW), (SCHG), (IUSG), and long global value (GVAL) as an equity barbell.

data.title: Schrödinger gets Fast Track status for SGR-1505
data.description:
• Schrödinger (NASDAQ:SDGR) said that the U.S. FDA granted Fast Track status to its MALT1 inhibitor, SGR-1505, for treating adults with Waldenström macroglobulinemia who have not responded to at least two previous therapies.
  • Fast-track designation allows a faster timeline for the development and review of drugs targeted at serious conditions with unmet medical needs.
  • It enables developers to communicate frequently with the regulator on plans for clinical studies.

data.title: United Natural Foods' cyberattack contained but will have 'material' impact on Q4 EPS
data.description:
Shares of United Natural Foods (UNFI were building on Thursday’s late gains after the company assured shareholders the June 5 cybersecurity event has been contained and is now regularly receiving and shipping products to retailers.

The company has also restored the core systems that customers and suppliers use for electronic ordering and invoicing, resulting in “normalized” business operations.

However, since the event resulted in reduced sales volume and increased operational costs, United Natural Foods (NYSE:UNFI) incurred -- and expects to continue to incur -- direct expenses related to the subsequent investigation and remediation of the incident.

“Based on the information currently available to the company, management believes that the incident is reasonably likely to have a material impact on net income/(loss) and adjusted EBITDA for the fourth fiscal quarter of 2025 compared to its internal projections prior to the incident,” United Natural Foods said in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Cybersecurity insurance is expected to be “adequate” and the full claim and settlement process will extend into FY26.  

Shares are up ~6%.

data.title: Meta's Zuckerberg, execs discuss 'de-investing' in Llama for competitors: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) executives, including CEO Mark Zuckerberg, have discussed “de-investing” in its Llama family of language models in favor of models made by competitors, The New York Times reported. 

Meta could look to models from OpenAI and Anthropic, the news outlet added, citing two people familiar with the matter. While no final decision has been made on the matter, it would mean turning towards the “closed” approach to models, whereas Llama is open source and can be tweaked by others. 

OpenAI is largely backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY), while Anthropic is backed by Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and others.

“We remain fully committed to developing Llama and plan to have multiple additional releases this year alone,” a Meta spokesperson told Seeking Alpha. OpenAI and Anthropic did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Meta released the first models from its Llama 4 series in April at its LlamaCon event, however the new model did not perform as well as rivals. Additionally, a number of developers left the event feeling underwhelmed, the news outlet added.

Since the release of Llama 4, Meta has gone on a spending spree to catch up with competitors.

Earlier this month, the company announced a deal to spend $14.3B to acquire nearly half of Scale AI. A key aspect of the deal includes Scale CEO Alexandr Wang joining Meta to head its “superintelligence” team focused on artificial general intelligence.

The company has also offered significant pay packages to bring AI researchers from OpenAI into Meta, including highly respected OpenAI researcher Trapit Bansal. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman recently said Meta was offering $100M signing bonuses in an attempt to poach his employees.

Meta is also reportedly in the final stages of acquiring PlayAI, a California-based startup that specializes in generating AI voices that are human-like.

The Zuckerberg-led Meta also reportedly discussed acquiring Perplexity AI prior to its multi-billion dollar investment into Scale AI. Apple (AAPL) has also reportedly discussed acquiring or partnering with Perplexity AI).

Deals with Runway AI and FuriosaAI were also considered, prior to Meta's Scale AI investment.

(This story has been updated with comment from Meta.)

data.title: US supreme court rules in favor of Obamacare task force
data.description:
• The U.S. Supreme Court on Friday ruled in favor of the Trump administration in a case that challenged the constitutionality of the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force, a key element introduced under the Affordable Care Act, also known as Obamacare, in 2010.
  • In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court justices struck down a lower court's ruling that determined that the USPSTF, which plays a key role in determining the type of preventive services health insurers must cover without cost to patients, was composed of invalid appointees.
  • Health insurers: UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Humana (HUM), CVS Health (NYSE:CVS),  Cigna (NYSE:CI), Clover Health (CLOV) and Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), Centene (CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV)

This is a developing story. Check back for more updates.

data.title: Estée Lauder gains after HSBC said the personal care stock has turned a corner
data.description:
The Estée Lauder Companies (NYSE:EL) rallied in late morning trading on Friday after HSBC upgraded the stock to a Buy rating after having it set at Hold. Importantly, the magnitude of the company's restructuring program and recent USD weakness is seen signaling an end to the earnings downgrade cycle.

Analyst Erwan Rambourg said there are clear reasons for investors to be positive on the cosmetics stick. "First, mechanically, like most US-based exporters, the group benefits from a weak USD. Second, and more structurally, the magnitude of the restructuring program, the so-called PRGP (profit recovery and growth plan), is such that margins should now have a solid floor starting this year (FY June 2026) even if sales growth doesn’t rebound sharply," highlighted Rambourg.

Notably, HSBC thinks there is evidence of market share gains for Estée Lauder (NYSE:EL) in key local markets such as the U.S., Japan, and China. On the fundamental side, EL is forecast to double EPS over the next two years, which makes valuation more attractive.

Estée Lauder (EL) has perked up over the last six weeks, with a rise of more than 25% as tariff anxiety from investors has ebbed. The stock still sits well below its 52-week high of $110.19. Estée Lauder (EL) is likely to issue its FQ4 report during the third week of August.
